from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about astronauts (or related entities) into a
    single fluent sentence. Uses predicate‑specific phrasing when possible
    and combines all clauses into one natural‑language sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Mapping of predicates to fragment templates (without repeating the subject).
    # These fragments will be combined per subject to form a single complex sentence.
    fragment_templates = {
        "almaMater": "studied at {object}",
        "birthDate": "was born on {object}",
        "birthName": "had the birth name {object}",
        "birthPlace": "was born in {object}",
        "dateOfRetirement": "retired in {object}",
        "occupation": "worked as a {object}",
        "status": "was {object}",
        "timeInSpace": "spent {object} minutes in space",
        "mission": "flew on the mission {object}",
        "selectedByNasa": "was selected by NASA in {object}",
        "deathDate": "died on {object}",
        "nationality": "was a citizen of {object}",
        "servedAsChiefOfTheAstronautOfficeIn": "served as chief of the astronaut office in {object}",
        "title": "held the title of {object}",
        "ribbonAward": "received the ribbon award {object}",
        "operator": "was operated by {object}",
        "backupPilot": "had {object} as backup pilot",
        "commander": "was commanded by {object}",
        "crewMembers": "had crew member {object}",
        "representative": "was represented by {object}",
        "alternativeName": "was also known as {object}",
        "awards": "received {object} awards",
        "award": "received the award {object}",
        "deathPlace": "died in {object}",
        "fossil": "is known for the fossil {object}",
        "senators": "is represented by senator {object}",
        "part": "is part of {object}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {object}",
        "bird": "has state bird {object}",
        "leader": "is led by {object}",
        "affiliation": "is affiliated with {object}",
        "competeIn": "competes in {object}",
        "president": "has president {object}",
        "gemstone": "has state gemstone {object}",
        "mascot": "has mascot {object}",
        "cosparId": "has COSPAR ID {object}",
        "utcOffset": "has UTC offset {object}",
        # Default fallback will be added later.
    }

    # Group fragments by subject to avoid repeating the subject in every clause.
    subject_fragments = {}
    for t in triples:
        obj = t.object.strip().strip('"')
        if t.predicate == "timeInSpace":
            try:
                minutes = float(obj.replace('"', '').replace("'", ""))
                hours = minutes / 60
                if hours.is_integer():
                    obj = f"{int(hours)} hours"
                else:
                    obj = f"{hours:.1f} hours"
            except ValueError:
                pass

        tmpl = fragment_templates.get(t.predicate)
        if tmpl:
            fragment = tmpl.format(object=obj)
        else:
            pred = t.predicate.replace("_", " ")
            fragment = f"{pred} {obj}"
        subject_fragments.setdefault(t.subject, []).append(fragment)

    # Build a sentence per subject, then join subjects together.
    subject_clauses = []
    for subj, frags in subject_fragments.items():
        if len(frags) == 1:
            clause = f"{subj} {frags[0]}"
        else:
            clause = f"{subj} " + ", ".join(frags[:-1]) + ", and " + frags[-1]
        subject_clauses.append(clause)

    # Combine all subject clauses into one final sentence.
    if len(subject_clauses) == 1:
        sentence = subject_clauses[0]
    elif len(subject_clauses) == 2:
        sentence = " and ".join(subject_clauses)
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(subject_clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + subject_clauses[-1]

    # Capitalize first character and ensure terminal punctuation.
    sentence = sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]
    if not sentence.endswith("."):
        sentence += "."
    return sentence

    clauses = []
    for t in triples:
        # Clean object string: remove surrounding quotes and whitespace
        obj = t.object.strip().strip('"')
        # Special handling for timeInSpace to convert minutes to hours for readability
        if t.predicate == "timeInSpace":
            try:
                minutes = float(obj.replace('"', '').replace("'", ""))
                hours = minutes / 60
                # Show with one decimal if not an integer
                if hours.is_integer():
                    obj = f"{int(hours)} hours"
                else:
                    obj = f"{hours:.1f} hours"
            except ValueError:
                pass  # keep original obj if conversion fails
        tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate)
        if tmpl:
            clause = tmpl.format(subject=t.subject, object=obj)
        else:
            # Fallback: make predicate readable
            pred = t.predicate.replace("_", " ")
            clause = f"{t.subject} {pred} {obj}"
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Combine clauses into a single fluent sentence using commas and conjunctions.
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0]
    elif len(clauses) == 2:
        sentence = " and ".join(clauses)
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1]

    # Ensure proper capitalization and punctuation.
    sentence = sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]
    if not sentence.endswith("."):
        sentence += "."
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
